article_meta.title: Federal immigration officers board an elevator with respondents at immigration court in New York on 17 November 2025

reasoning: The summary avoids the article’s loaded framing (e.g., replaces “spied” with “accessed”) and attributes evaluative claims to sources. It quotes charged labels only with attribution and notes lack of evidence as reported. Minor imbalance: it omits the DOJ’s pro-enforcement statement from the summary/key facts, though it lists DOJ in sources.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs like “accessed,” “characterized,” and attributes condemnations.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: All contested claims are attributed; notes lack of evidence per the report.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Subjective judgments (e.g., criminalizing protected activity) are clearly attributed to advocates.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Avoids charged terms like “spied,” “abductions,” and dramatic metaphors present in the article.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks in the model’s voice.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about motives without attribution.
custom_reasoning.slant: Slight emphasis on critics; NYPD view included, but DOJ’s defense is not summarized.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: DOJ spokesperson’s pro-enforcement statement is omitted from summary/key facts.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically neutral vocabulary throughout; charged labels only in quotes with attribution.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Tone is balanced compared to the source’s strongly critical framing.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and activist sources with similar neutrality.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Materially more neutral than the article, avoiding loaded framing.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Most perspectives represented, but DOJ counterpoint not included in narrative or key facts.
